behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing, and missing fields are all reported.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between validation and the intake orchestrator: you must prove downstream modules are not invoked when fields are missing, while also checking aggregated error reporting. A pure unit test of the validator alone can miss orchestration leaks.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake entrypoint with missing `name`, `date_of_birth`, and `insurance_id`; assert response includes all missing-field names and assert insurance/triage/scheduling/confirmation collaborators were not called.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is local validation logic with no required module interaction or I/O. Real failures are in parsing/range checks inside one function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Test DOB validator directly with representative invalid inputs (future date, >150 years old, malformed string) and valid boundary values; assert specific rejection reasons.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code and maps eligible/not eligible/pending correctly.
behaviors.justification: The bug surface is request construction + response mapping across internal modules and the payer client boundary. Real external payer calls are not strictly required to catch payload/mapping defects.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise eligibility service with a fake payer client (or local stub) and assert outbound payload fields (`patient_id`, `plan_code`) and mapping for `eligible`, `not_eligible`, `pending` responses.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: The critical failure is error propagation between payer client and orchestration flow. Simulating transport failure at the client boundary is sufficient to verify fallback and continuation behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Force payer client call to fail with timeout/connection error, then run intake orchestration; assert eligibility becomes `pending` and downstream triage/scheduling/confirmation still execute.

behaviors.description: AI symptom triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment quality, which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated by a deterministic mock without hiding true failures.
behaviors.test_description: Send emergency symptom cases to the real triage model API across repeated runs; assert high-urgency classification rate meets threshold and flag drift/regressions.

behaviors.description: Symptom triage returns a clinician-reviewable natural-language explanation, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: The required behavior is generation quality/structure from the real AI engine. Mocked text would trivially pass and hide real output-shape or coherence failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call real triage model with representative symptoms; assert response includes urgency plus non-empty clinician-facing explanation meeting basic readability/medical-rationale format checks.

behaviors.description: Scheduling chooses first available slot matching urgency SLA (same-day for high, within 48h for medium).
behaviors.justification: This depends on scheduler logic interacting with calendar persistence/query module. A unit test can miss defects in slot retrieval/order and persisted availability state.
behaviors.test_description: With seeded local calendar DB data, run scheduler for high/medium urgency inputs; assert selected slot is the earliest that satisfies each urgency window.

behaviors.description: Scheduling prevents double-booking under concurrent patient processing.
behaviors.justification: The real failure emerges from concurrent interactions with shared calendar DB state (locking/transactions). Single-function tests cannot expose race conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests against the same candidate slot in a local DB with real transaction semantics; assert only one booking succeeds and no duplicate slot row/state appears.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message contains patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is template/composition logic in one local boundary. No real I/O or multi-module interaction is required to catch missing fields in message rendering.
behaviors.test_description: Test confirmation renderer/composer with appointment and visit-type fixtures; assert output includes required tokens and correct preparation text per visit type.

behaviors.description: Full intake pipeline (validate → insurance → triage → schedule → confirm) completes automatically for a real submission.
behaviors.justification: This end-to-end completion includes a real AI step whose live output can break downstream parsing/flow; deterministic stubs can hide this. It also spans external/system boundaries, but the minimum level is driven by real model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end intake with real submission against production-like wiring, including real payer call path and real triage model; assert terminal state is successful confirmation without manual intervention.
